title: Past continuous and past simple
explanation:
Past continuous and past simple

Past continuous (was/were + -ing) — longer action in progress in the past.

Past simple — finished action that interrupts it.

I was watching TV when the phone rang.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.text: They [*were playing|played|play] football at three o'clock.
She [*wasn't listening|didn't listen|not listened] — she was on her phone.
He [*broke|was breaking|break] his arm while he was skiing.
It [*was raining|rained|rain] so we stayed inside.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: They ____ TV when the lights went out. | *were watching | watched | watch
She ____ home when she saw the accident. | *was walking | walked | walks
I ____ him while I was waiting for the bus. | *met | was meeting | meet
At eight o'clock, they ____ football. | *were playing | played | play

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.text: We [*were having|had|have] dinner when the news started.
I [*saw|was seeing|see] a friend while I was shopping.
He [*was driving|drove|drive] too fast and crashed.
They [*were talking|talked|talk] about the project all morning.
